title: Volumes Hold Up as Prices Drift: Iron Ore's First Half in Review
section_path: Volumes Hold Up as Prices Drift: Iron Ore's First Half in Review
section_path_text: Volumes Hold Up as Prices Drift: Iron Ore's First Half in Review
summary:
Main
By Ulf Bergman
After another year of growth in 2025, global seaborne iron ore exports maintained positive momentum in the first half of this year, although uncertainties emerged as the year progressed. Meanwhile, iron ore prices have seen some fluctuations, but they have stayed within typical levels and a narrow range. In addition to the modest growth, the past six months have brought a novelty, as exports from Guinea's Simandou mine have begun to reach the global marketplace.

children.title: Main
children.summary:
By Ulf Bergman
After another year of growth in 2025, global seaborne iron ore exports maintained positive momentum in the first half of this year, although uncertainties emerged as the year progressed. Meanwhile, iron ore prices have seen some fluctuations, but they have stayed within typical levels and a narrow range.

children.title: Iron Ore Price Volatility but Nothing Exceptional
section_path: Iron Ore Price Volatility but Nothing Exceptional
children.section_path_text: Iron Ore Price Volatility but Nothing Exceptional
children.summary: Over the past six months, iron ore prices have fluctuated, but the magnitude has been far from exceptional compared with recent years. The August SGX Iron Ore IODEX futures have traded between 95 and 110 dollars per tonne during the first half of the year, ending June just shy of 99 dollars.

children.title: Iron Ore Flows During the First Half of the Year
section_path: Iron Ore Flows During the First Half of the Year
children.section_path_text: Iron Ore Flows During the First Half of the Year
children.summary: Global seaborne iron ore exports rose by 2.3 per cent in the first half of the year, compared with the same period in 2025, reaching nearly 852 million tonnes, according to Signal Ocean data. Although the year-on-year growth fell short of last year's 3.6 per cent, a closer look at the data shows that the annual expansion in volumes was confined to the second half of the year.

children.title: Outlook for the Second Half of the Year
section_path: Outlook for the Second Half of the Year
children.section_path_text: Outlook for the Second Half of the Year
children.summary: The recent uptick in Chinese steel rebar prices amid improving data may provide an early indication of what lies ahead for global seaborne iron ore volumes over the coming six months. However, it is important to treat the recent developments with caution and a healthy dose of scepticism, as there have been false dawns aplenty.
